# chronyd-rs

The daemon / replay binary of [chrony-rs](../../README.md). It hosts the
[`chrony-rs-core`](../chrony-rs-core/README.md) brain in **lab and offline modes
only** — it does not discipline a real system clock or open production sockets.

This file is **generated** (`cargo xtask gen`) and freshness-gated.

## What it does

- `chronyd-rs --check-config <file>` — parse and validate a chrony config the way
chrony 4.5 does (acceptance + diagnostics oracle-witnessed; see
[`docs/config-atlas.md`](../../docs/config-atlas.md)).
- `chronyd-rs --replay <trace.json>` — drive a validated deterministic trace
through a simulated clock and emit a reproducible decision-log hash.

## What it deliberately does NOT do

No real clock step/slew, no NTP serving, no live control socket — the boundaries
are enumerated in [`docs/negative-capabilities.md`](../../docs/negative-capabilities.md)
(itself generated). Targets chrony 4.5.
